channel.title: AI Radar - 每日情报摘要
channel.description: AI 产品设计雷达 - 自动化追踪 AI 领域最新技术、产品、商业动态

image.title: AI Radar - 每日情报摘要

item.title: Show HN: Explore color palettes inspired by 3000 master painter artworks
Estimating the expected output of wide random MLPs more efficiently than samplin

description.p: By far the most common way to estimate an expected loss in machine learning is to draw samples, compute the loss on each one, and take the empirical average. However, sampling is not necessarily optimal. Given an MLP at initialization, we show how to estimate its expected output over Gaussian inputs

item.title: When Life Gives You BC, Make Q-functions: Extracting Q-values from Behavior Clon

description.p: Behavior Cloning (BC) has emerged as a highly effective paradigm for robot learning. However, BC lacks a self-guided mechanism for online improvement after demonstrations have been collected. Existing offline-to-online learning methods often cause policies to replace previously learned good actions

item.title: Geometry-Aware State Space Model: A New Paradigm for Whole-Slide Image Represent

description.p: Accurate analysis of histopathological images is critical for disease diagnosis and treatment planning. Whole-slide images (WSIs), which digitize tissue specimens at gigapixel resolution, are fundamental to this process but require aggregating thousands of patches for slide-level predictions. Multip

item.title: Understanding In-Context Learning for Nonlinear Regression with Transformers: At

description.p: Pre-trained transformers are able to learn from examples provided as part of the prompt without any weight updates, a remarkable ability known as in-context learning (ICL). Despite its demonstrated efficacy across various domains, the theoretical understanding of ICL is still developing. Whereas mos

item.title: Taming Outlier Tokens in Diffusion Transformers

description.p: We study outlier tokens in Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) for image generation. Prior work has shown that Vision Transformers (ViTs) can produce a small number of high-norm tokens that attract disproportionate attention while carrying limited local information, but their role in generative models rem

item.title: Design Conductor 2.0: An agent builds a TurboQuant inference accelerator in 80 h

description.p: Driven by a rapid co-evolution of both harness and underlying models, LLM agents are improving at a dizzying pace. In our prior work (performed in Dec. 2025), we introduced "Design Conductor" (or just "Conductor"), a system capable of building a 5-stage Linux-capable RISC-V CPU in 12 hours. In this

item.title: PSK at SemEval-2026 Task 9: Multilingual Polarization Detection Using Ensemble G

description.p: We present our system for SemEval-2026 Task 9: Multilingual Polarization Detection, a binary classification task spanning 22 languages. Our approach fine-tunes separate Gemma~3 models (12B and 27B parameters) per language using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), augmented with synthetic data generated by a

item.title: Sharp Capacity Thresholds in Linear Associative Memory: From Winner-Take-All to

description.p: How many key-value associations can a $d\times d$ linear memory store? We show that the answer depends not only on the $d^2$ degrees of freedom in the memory matrix, but also on the retrieval criterion. In an isotropic Gaussian model for the stored pairs, we show that top-1 retrieval, where every si

item.title: The First Token Knows: Single-Decode Confidence for Hallucination Detection

description.p: Self-consistency detects hallucinations by generating multiple sampled answers to a question and measuring agreement, but this requires repeated decoding and can be sensitive to lexical variation. Semantic self-consistency improves this by clustering sampled answers by meaning using natural language

item.title: LongSeeker: Elastic Context Orchestration for Long-Horizon Search Agents

description.p: Long-horizon search agents must manage a rapidly growing working context as they reason, call tools, and observe information. Naively accumulating all intermediate content can overwhelm the agent, increasing costs and the risk of errors. We propose that effective context management should be adaptiv

item.title: SQLite Is a Library of Congress Recommended Storage Format

description.p: 这是一个 Hacker News 热门讨论，反映了社区对该话题的关注度。

item.title: Show HN: I built an open-source email builder, alternative to Beefree/Unlayer

description.p: 这是一个 Hacker News 热门讨论，反映了社区对该话题的关注度。

item.title: A Theory of Deep Learning

description.p: 这是一个 Hacker News 热门讨论，反映了社区对该话题的关注度。

item.title: Snap says its $400M deal with Perplexity ‘amicably ended’

description.p: The deal, announced last November, would have seen Perplexity's AI search engine integrated directly into Snapchat.

item.title: Khosla-backed robotics startup Genesis AI has gone full stack, demo shows

description.p: Genesis AI, a startup that raised a $105 million seed round to build foundational AI for robotics, has unveiled its first model, GENE-26.5, but also a demo showcasing a set of robotic hands performing complex tasks.

item.title: How Elon Musk left OpenAI, according to Greg Brockman

description.p: Cutthroat negotiations between startup founders are rarely shared so publicly, especially when a company becomes as world-changing as OpenAI.

item.title: Is xAI a neocloud now?

description.p: xAI's real business may be more about building data centers than training AI models.

item.title: Learning the Integral of a Diffusion Model

description.p: 这是一个 Hacker News 热门讨论，反映了社区对该话题的关注度。

item.title: AI boom pushes Samsung to $1T

description.p: Samsung crossed the $1 trillion valuation mark after shares surged on AI-driven chip demand, making it only the second Asian company after TSMC to hit the milestone.

item.title: Ethos raises $22.75M from a16z for its expert network with voice onboarding

description.p: Ethos says it is onboarding 35,000 experts per week.

item.title: Apple is enforcing an old App Store rule against a new kind of software

description.p: 这是一个 Hacker News 热门讨论，反映了社区对该话题的关注度。

item.title: DeepSeek could hit $45B valuation from its first investment round

description.p: The Chinese AI lab came to prominence in early 2025 after launching a large language model that trained on a fraction of the compute power and at a fraction of the cost of the big U.S. models like those from OpenAI and Anthropic.

item.title: Google updates AI search to include quotes from Reddit and other sources

description.p: While citing web forums and discussion boards can help users find answers to more niche queries, this design choice could also prove chaotic.

item.title: Almost-Orthogonality in Lp Spaces: A Case Study with Grok

description.p: Carbery proposed the following sharpened form of triangle inequality for many functions: for any $p\ge 2$ and any finite sequence $(f_j)_j\subset L^p$ we have \[ \Big\|\sum_j f_j\Big\|_p \ \le\ \left(\sup_{j} \sum_{k} α_{jk}^{\,c}\right)^{1/p'} \Big(\sum_j \|f_j\|_p^p\Big)^{1/p}, \] where $c=2$, $1/

item.title: Grokability in five inequalities

description.p: In this note, we report five mathematical discoveries made in collaboration with Grok, all of which have been subsequently verified by the authors. These include an improved lower bound on the maximal Gaussian perimeter of convex sets in $\mathbb{R}^n$, sharper $L_2$-$L_1$ moment comparison inequali

item.title: Barry Diller trusts Sam Altman. But ‘trust is irrelevant’ as AGI nea

description.p: Barry Diller defended OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, while warning that AGI remains an unpredictable force needing guardrails.

item.title: Apple to pay $250M to settle lawsuit over Siri’s delayed AI features

description.p: Apple has agreed to pay $250 million to settle a class action lawsuit for overpromising the arrival of Siri's AI features.

item.title: Tinder owner Match Group is slowing hiring to pay for its increased use of AI to

description.p: Match Group said that it's slowing its hiring plans for the rest of the year because AI tools "cost a lot of money."

item.title: Five architects of the AI economy explain where the wheels are coming off

description.p: Earlier this week, five people who touch every layer of the AI supply chain sat down at the Milken Global Conference in Beverly Hills, where they talked with TechCrunch about everything from chip shortages to orbital data centers to the possibility that the whole architecture that undergirds the tec

item.title: 3 days left to lock in 50% off a second ticket to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026

description.p: Three days left to lock in 50% off a second ticket to Disrupt 2026. Buy one TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket, and get a second ticket at 50% off. Gain more visibility in the tech industry. Offer ends May 8 at 11:59 p.m. PT.

item.title: At TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, all your M&A questions will be answered

description.p: Leaders from Coinbase, M13, and Mignano Law Group talk about how M&A is an early-stage strategy at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Register to hear this live.

item.title: SpaceX may spend up to $119B on ‘Terafab’ chip factory in Texas

description.p: The project would be a "multi-phase, next-generation, vertically integrated semiconductor manufacturing and advanced computing fabrication facility," according to the proposal.

item.title: Flow Sampling: Learning to Sample from Unnormalized Densities via Denoising Cond

description.p: Sampling from unnormalized densities is analogous to the generative modeling problem, but the target distribution is defined by a known energy function instead of data samples. Because evaluating the energy function is often costly, a primary challenge is to learn an efficient sampler. We introduce

item.title: Large-Scale High-Quality 3D Gaussian Head Reconstruction from Multi-View Capture

description.p: We propose HeadsUp, a scalable feed-forward method for reconstructing high-quality 3D Gaussian heads from large-scale multi-camera setups. Our method employs an efficient encoder-decoder architecture that compresses input views into a compact latent representation. This latent representation is then

item.title: From Intent to Execution: Composing Agentic Workflows with Agent Recommendation

description.p: Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) built using AI agents fulfill a variety of user intents that may be used to design and build a family of related applications. However, the creation of such MAS currently involves manual composition of the plan, manual selection of appropriate agents, and manual creation of

item.title: OpenSeeker-v2: Pushing the Limits of Search Agents with Informative and High-Dif

description.p: Deep search capabilities have become an indispensable competency for frontier Large Language Model (LLM) agents, yet their development remains dominated by industrial giants. The typical industry recipe involves a highly resource-intensive pipeline spanning pre-training, continual pre-training (CPT)

item.title: A Closed-Form Adaptive-Landmark Kernel for Certified Point-Cloud and Graph Class

description.p: We introduce PALACE (Persistence Adaptive-Landmark Analytic Classification Engine), the data-adaptive companion to PLACE, paying a small cross-validation tier on three knobs (budget, radii, bandwidth; $\leq 5$ choices each). A cover-theoretic core (Lebesgue-number criterion on the landmark cover) yi

item.title: Redefining AI Red Teaming in the Agentic Era: From Weeks to Hours

description.p: AI systems are entering critical domains like healthcare, finance, and defense, yet remain vulnerable to adversarial attacks. While AI red teaming is a primary defense, current approaches force operators into manual, library-specific workflows. Operators spend weeks hand-crafting workflows - assembl

item.title: SymptomAI: Towards a Conversational AI Agent for Everyday Symptom Assessment

description.p: Language models excel at diagnostic assessments on currated medical case-studies and vignettes, performing on par with, or better than, clinical professionals. However, existing studies focus on complex scenarios with rich context making it difficult to draw conclusions about how these systems perfo

item.title: Physics-Grounded Multi-Agent Architecture for Traceable, Risk-Aware Human-AI Dec

description.p: High-precision CNC machining of free-form aerospace components requires bounded compensations informed by inspection, simulation, and process knowledge. Off-the-shelf large language model (LLM) assistants can generate text, but they do not reliably execute risk-constrained multi-step numerical workf

item.title: Enhanced 3D Brain Tumor Segmentation Using Assorted Precision Training

description.p: A brain tumor is a medical disorder faced by individuals of all demographics. Medically, it is described as the spread of non-essential cells close to or throughout the brain. Symptoms of this ailment include headaches, seizures, and sensory changes. This research explores two main categories of bra

item.title: An Agent-Oriented Pluggable Experience-RAG Skill for Experience-Driven Retrieval

description.p: Retrieval-augmented generation systems often assume that one fixed retrieval pipeline is sufficient across heterogeneous tasks, yet factoid question answering, multi-hop reasoning, and scientific verification exhibit different retrieval preferences. We present Experience-RAG Skill, an agent-oriented

item.title: Conditional Diffusion Sampling

description.p: Sampling from unnormalized multimodal distributions with limited density evaluations remains a fundamental challenge in machine learning and natural sciences. Successful approaches construct a bridge between a tractable reference and the target distribution. Parallel Tempering (PT) serves as the gol

item.title: Safety and accuracy follow different scaling laws in clinical large language mod

description.p: Clinical LLMs are often scaled by increasing model size, context length, retrieval complexity, or inference-time compute, with the implicit expectation that higher accuracy implies safer behavior. This assumption is incomplete in medicine, where a few confident, high-risk, or evidence-contradicting
